questions.answer:
B. a d
   b e
   c f
questions.to_find: Contents of the 3x2 array b after the nested loop fills it via pointer arithmetic

understand.plain: A 2x3 character array 'a' is defined with values a..f. A 3x2 array 'b' is declared and a pointer p points to its first element. A nested loop copies elements of 'a' into 'b' using the formula *(p + 2*j + i) = a[i][j]. We need to find the final layout of array b.

keywords.explain: Using integer offsets from a base pointer to access different memory locations; *(p + k) accesses the k-th byte from where p points.
keywords.example: If p = &b[0][0], then *(p+2) = b[1][0] for a 3x2 array stored in row-major order.

keywords.explain: C stores 2D arrays row by row in contiguous memory; b[r][c] is at offset r*num_cols + c from the base.

understand.visual_alt: Source array a[2][3] with values a,b,c in row 0 and d,e,f in row 1. Destination array b[3][2] filled column-by-column: row 0 gets (a,d), row 1 gets (b,e), row 2 gets (c,f).

given.aim: Determine the final contents of b[3][2] after the nested loop copies a[2][3] using pointer offset 2*j + i.

terms.connects: Values being assigned into b via the formula

terms.meaning: Pointer to first element b[0][0]; b laid out in memory as b[0][0],b[0][1],b[1][0],b[1][1],b[2][0],b[2][1] at offsets 0..5
terms.connects: The base address used for all pointer arithmetic writes

terms.meaning: The memory offset into b where a[i][j] is written; effectively transposes a into b column-by-column
terms.connects: Determines which cell of b receives each character from a

given.plan: Enumerate all (i,j) pairs from the loop, compute offset = 2*j + i for each, and record which cell of b (row = offset/2, col = offset%2) receives a[i][j]. Then read off the 3x2 layout of b.

steps.title: Map memory layout of b[3][2]
steps.formula_raw: b[r][c] is at memory offset r*2 + c from p
steps.note: b has 3 rows and 2 cols, so the stride (num_cols) is 2

steps.title: Apply the assignment formula for each (i,j)
steps.note: The formula 2*j + i swaps the roles of the row and column indices, effectively transposing a into b

steps.title: Read final layout of b[3][2]
steps.note: This is the transpose of a: rows of a become columns of b

solution.result: B. The contents of b are: row0=(a,d), row1=(b,e), row2=(c,f)

questions.formula_note: Row-major memory layout of 2D arrays in C; pointer arithmetic with offset = 2*j + i transposes a 2x3 matrix into a 3x2 matrix.

questions.to_find: The exact output printed when parameters are evaluated right to left

understand.plain: The program calls f(g(10), h(20)) where parameters are evaluated right to left. Inside f, g(x) and h(y) are called sequentially. Each of g and h prints its argument. We need to trace all printf calls in order to find the final output string.

keywords.explain: The order in which actual arguments of a function call are computed before the function executes. C leaves this unspecified, but this question fixes it to right-to-left.
keywords.example: f(g(10), h(20)) right-to-left: h(20) is evaluated first, then g(10).

keywords.explain: printf inside g and h fires as a side effect when those functions are called to compute the argument values, before f even starts.
keywords.example: h(20) prints '20' as a side effect while computing the argument for f's second parameter.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='500' height='200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='120' height='40' class='hi'/><text x='70' y='35' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>h(20) eval</text><text x='70' y='55' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>prints: 20</text><rect x='150' y='10' width='120' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='210' y='35' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>g(10) eval</text><text x='210' y='55' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>prints: 10</text><rect x='290' y='10' width='120' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='350' y='35' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>f called</text><text x='350' y='55' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>x=10, y=20</text><rect x='10' y='100' width='120' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='70' y='125' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>g(x)=g(10)</text><text x='70' y='145' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>prints: 10</text><rect x='150' y='100' width='120' height='40' class='bg'/><text x='210' y='125' class='lbl ink' text-anchor='middle'>h(y)=h(20)</text><text x='210' y='145' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>prints: 20</text><text x='10' y='185' class='lbl ink'>Final output: 20 10 10 20  =>  20101020</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Execution timeline: Step 1 h(20) prints 20, Step 2 g(10) prints 10 (right-to-left argument evaluation), then inside f: g(10) prints 10, h(20) prints 20. Total output: 20101020.

given.aim: Trace all printf calls in order given right-to-left argument evaluation to find the complete output string.

terms.meaning: The rightmost argument h(20) is evaluated first, then g(10), before f executes.
terms.example: h(20) runs first: prints '20', returns 20. Then g(10) runs: prints '10', returns 10.
terms.connects: These two prints happen before f's body executes at all.

terms.meaning: After right-to-left evaluation: x = g(10) = 10, y = h(20) = 20.
terms.example: x=10, y=20 are passed to f.
terms.connects: f then calls g(x)=g(10) and h(y)=h(20), generating two more prints.

terms.term: sequential calls inside f: g(x); h(y);
terms.meaning: f calls g with x then h with y, each printing its argument.
terms.connects: These are the 3rd and 4th printf calls.

given.plan: List all printf calls in chronological order: (1) h(20) as right arg of f, (2) g(10) as left arg of f, (3) g(x) inside f, (4) h(y) inside f. Concatenate the printed values.

steps.title: Evaluate arguments of f right to left
steps.apply:
h(20) is evaluated: printf("%d", 20) prints '20', returns 20.
g(10) is evaluated: printf("%d", 10) prints '10', returns 10.
Now f is called with x=10, y=20.

steps.title: Execute f body: g(x); h(y);
steps.apply:
g(x) = g(10): printf("%d", 10) prints '10', returns 10.
h(y) = h(20): printf("%d", 20) prints '20', returns 20.

steps.title: Combine all printed values
steps.apply:
Print sequence: 20 (from h(20) arg eval), 10 (from g(10) arg eval), 10 (from g(x) in f), 20 (from h(y) in f).
Final output string: 20101020
steps.note: No spaces or newlines are printed since the format string is '%d' with no separators.

questions.formula_note: C argument evaluation order is implementation-defined; here fixed to right-to-left — rightmost actual argument expression is fully evaluated (including side effects) before the one to its left.
